decision.archive_summary: Earlier block covered verifying/testing and refining the UX requirement for context-size visibility: the user wants context size shown directly from the VS Code status-bar flow, with continuous automatic updates and no manual command refresh. That exploratory implementation path was later superseded by a boundary correction: when the assistant linked it to Project Assistant, the user required a standalone solution with clear separation and no touching Project Assistant.
decision.reasoning_summary: The active topic is still the same implementation thread, now narrowed to the boundary requirement around a standalone extension, so relation=continue. Raw turns t9-t12 are the critical evidence for the current state and should remain. Turns t1-t8 are older and mostly resolved/status-oriented; their durable product requirements can survive as pins instead of raw prompt turns. Pin t11 because the boundary rule is durable and likely to matter in subsequent turns; pin t3 and t5 because they contain the core UX requirement for direct, automatic, continuously visible context-size display.
